title: Mildly Bitter Ethiopian Beef Kitfo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring tender beef, chilled butter, and a hint of bitterness, made ovo-vegetarian-friendly by using plant-based alternatives to traditional ingredients.
instructions: Grind the mitmita spice into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
In a large bowl, combine the ground mitmita spice, chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger.
Add the lean beef pieces to the bowl and mix well to coat with the spice mixture.
Heat the tadka oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef mixture to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the chilled butter until it is fully incorporated.
Season the kitfo with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the kitfo chilled, garnished with additional mitmita spice if desired.
